subject: english
alignment_note: CPP L1 topic vocabulary maps directly to lower-primary vocabulary development through experience and meaningful use; Foundation naming becomes broader contextual word use.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed child recognizes names or uses target words with meaning shown by picture object gesture or sentence.

subject: english
alignment_note: China Level 1 vocabulary grammar emphasizes sound form meaning and use; grade band is a later primary reference so evidence must remain support-aware.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Dated word-use sample showing whether response was recognition repetition prompted naming or independent use.

subject: english
alignment_note: CPP L1 sentence patterns fit lower-primary word phrase and sentence grammar in meaningful contexts; Foundation frames expand into more varied grammar.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Child adapts a sentence frame with meaningful substitution and shows what the sentence means.

subject: english
alignment_note: PYP Phase 2 writing supports simple sentence structures but is broader than oral frame work; useful when written or built sentences are observed.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Short written built or dictated sentence with support level and child contribution recorded.

subject: english
alignment_note: CPP L1 language functions align with simple exchanges personal information feelings preferences and short supported language; Foundation interaction becomes purposeful exchange.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observation of the communicative purpose and exact child utterance or response.

subject: english
alignment_note: Singapore speaking and representing supports purposeful communication and respectful discussion; CPP L1 provides unit-level communicative routines.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed child asks answers clarifies describes or explains in a short exchange with prompt level recorded.

subject: english
alignment_note: Singapore lower-primary foundation explicitly emphasizes phonemic awareness phonics and early literacy; CPP L1 has unit sound-pattern records.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Child identifies sorts repeats or reads target sound-pattern words in a visible task.

subject: english
alignment_note: PYP Phase 2 reading includes decoding strategies and printed-code awareness; alignment is broader than individual CPP phonics entries.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Evidence that the child used a sound or print clue rather than only echoing the adult.

subject: english
alignment_note: CPP L1 listening tasks map to attentive listening and viewing response through contextual clues; Foundation listen-and-act develops into comprehension response.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observation of action choice drawing note or short answer after a specific listening or viewing prompt.

subject: english
alignment_note: China preparatory and Level 1 bridge supports listening speaking input and routine interaction; it is broader and less task-specific than CPP L1 listening items.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Record exact prompt language and whether translation gesture or visual choices were used.

subject: english
alignment_note: CPP L1 speaking/oracy aligns with Singapore lower-primary speaking to fulfill purposes; Foundation one-word/frame response becomes short supported speech.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Transcript or close paraphrase of child response plus support level.

subject: english
alignment_note: PYP oral language Phase 1 to 2 bridge fits personal-experience speech questions turn-taking and increasingly accurate sounds or grammar.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed spoken response retell or explanation connected to a picture object or familiar context.

subject: english
alignment_note: CPP L1 reading strategies map to beginning reading and viewing through prediction contextual clues and comprehension; Foundation visual-reading readiness becomes explicit strategy use.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed child points to a clue predicts sequences or explains a reading choice.

subject: english
alignment_note: PYP Phase 2 reading includes shared/guided reading prediction connection and decoding; strong fit for L1 strategy observation.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Child uses a text picture or print clue and explains meaning with prompt level recorded.

subject: english
alignment_note: Singapore includes accuracy and fluency but CPP L1 evidence should remain readiness-level unless independent sustained reading is observed.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed familiar word caption or repeated line recognition with print tracking or sound clue.

subject: english
alignment_note: Early literacy and phonics support fluency readiness; do not overclaim independent fluency from supported recognition.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Record word set text length and whether adult reading model was present.

subject: english
alignment_note: CPP L1 writing readiness aligns with lower-primary print script spelling and early idea expression; Foundation tracing/copying moves into meaningful labels and captions.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Child-created label caption sentence or short description with support type recorded.

subject: english
alignment_note: PYP Phase 2 writing supports simple sentences personal ideas captions and rereading for meaning; strong fit for observation-ready L1 writing.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Work sample or parent note showing traced copied selected dictated jointly composed or independent contribution.

subject: english
alignment_note: PYP Phase 2 visual language maps directly to connecting visual information with experience and communicating through media; CPP L1 uses pictures and captions heavily.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Child explains how picture symbol or layout helps meaning or creates a visual-language response.

subject: english
alignment_note: Singapore listening/viewing supports multimodal comprehension but CPP visual creation tasks are narrower than the standard.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observed link between visual cue and English word sentence or response.

subject: english
alignment_note: Singapore lower-primary discussion and respectful exchange align with CPP L1 interaction cues; Foundation greetings and turn-taking become short discussion moves.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observation of turn-taking asking clarification responding or feedback in a short exchange.

subject: english
alignment_note: PYP oral Phase 1 to 2 bridge includes turn-taking questions feelings opinions and relationship-building interactions.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Record the exact interaction move and whether it was initiated prompted or rehearsed.

subject: english
alignment_note: China learning strategy requirements align with active participation support-seeking and reflection; this objective protects evidence quality for L1 practice.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Short practice record with task date child action support level and reflection.

subject: english
alignment_note: Singapore integration of receptive/productive skills and positive dispositions supports home evidence routines but does not define progress ledger rules directly.
evidence_needed_for_progress: Observation-ready sample showing what child said did selected read wrote or asked.
